about.me

Who's Yelling?

omya_profile.sh
$ whoaminame: Old Man Yelling Atrole: Data Scientist / AI Curmudgeonstatus: Perpetually annoyed, highly effective$ cat skills.txtlanguages: Python, SQLhardware: Custom builds & tinkeringviz: Tableau, Apache Supersetai/ml: LLMs, Yelling (advanced)$ echo $PHILOSOPHY"If the data is wrong, I will find it."$ _

Professionally Irate,
Technically Competent

I turn messy, broken, lying data into dashboards that people actually use — and then yell about the ones they don't. With a background spanning SQL, Python, hardware, and the full data stack, I build things from the database up.

My specialty is finding the question nobody asked that explains everything. Also yelling at clouds. Figuratively. Mostly.

Currently experimenting with LLMs to see if I can outsource the yelling. Results: inconclusive. The LLM is too polite.

OMYA //

home / projects / project-name
Complete

Those Planes That Fly
Too Damn Low

An evidence-based platform for proving, conclusively, that yes — those planes are flying too low.
TypeData Analysis / Visualisation
StackPython · SQL · Apache Superset
Year2026
Repogithub.com/omya →

The problem was simple: planes are flying too damn low over residential areas, too frequently, and at antisocial hours. The question was how to prove it.

This project ingests ADS-B flight data from a local receiver, cleans and enriches it with route and aircraft metadata, then surfaces it through a live Superset dashboard — with altitude distributions, frequency heatmaps, and worst-offender rankings.

The Problem

Most flight-tracking tools are built for enthusiasts, not complainers. None of them let you ask "how often does something fly under 2,000ft over my exact location between 6am and 8am on weekdays?" This does.

The Approach

  • Deployed a Raspberry Pi with an SDR receiver to capture ADS-B transponder signals
  • Ingested raw data into PostgreSQL via a Python ETL pipeline running on a schedule
  • Enriched with aircraft registration data and route lookups
  • Built a Superset dashboard with altitude filters, time-of-day slices, and a heat map

Tech Stack

PythonSQLPostgreSQLSupersetRaspberry PiADS-B

Project Stats

Flights tracked47,200+
Data range6 months
Offenders foundYes
Complaint filedAlso yes
screenshots

In Action

[ Dashboard screenshot goes here ]
Replace this div with an <img> tag pointing to your screenshot
results

What I Found

The data confirmed what the ears already knew. Peak low-altitude activity was between 7:15am and 8:30am, with a secondary spike around 5pm. Three operators accounted for 60% of all sub-2,000ft passes.

340
Low passes per month
3
Worst offenders
1,200ft
Lowest recorded
6am
Earliest wake-up call
learnings

What I Learned

  • ADS-B data is messier than it looks — deduplication and trajectory reconstruction took most of the pipeline time
  • Superset's SQL Lab is genuinely excellent for rapid iteration on dashboard queries
  • Having the data doesn't automatically win the argument — but it helps
All ProjectsJack's Grapes

© 2026 Old Man Yelling At — Data Science, AI & Cantankerousness

The Arsenal

Tools I use, abuse, and occasionally yell at
PythonExpert
pandas, polars, scikit-learn, FastAPI. If it can be done in Python, it probably should be.
pandaspolarsscikit-learnFastAPI
SQLExpert
CTEs, window functions, query optimisation, the dark arts. PostgreSQL primarily, but I speak most dialects.
PostgreSQLDuckDBBigQuery
TableauAdvanced
Dashboards that actually communicate something. Calculated fields, LOD expressions, and the occasional strongly-worded tooltip.
Tableau DesktopTableau Server
Apache SupersetAdvanced
Open-source BI that doesn't cost an arm and a leg. Self-hosted, customised, and hooked up to everything.
SupersetSQL LabDruid
LLMs & AIAdvanced
Prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, fine-tuning, and the philosophical question of whether the model is actually thinking.
OpenAIAnthropicLangChainRAG
Data EngineeringAdvanced
ETL/ELT pipelines, data modelling, orchestration. Building the pipes so the dashboards don't lie.
dbtAirflowParquet
Hardware & BuildsProficient
Custom PC builds, Raspberry Pi projects, edge compute, and the satisfying click of a good mechanical keyboard.
Raspberry PiCustom PCEdge Compute
Machine LearningAdvanced
Classification, regression, clustering. Knowing when a simple model is better than a flashy one is half the battle.
scikit-learnXGBoostMLflow
YellingExpert
At clouds. At bad dashboards. At planes flying too low. At Y-axes that don't start at zero. Core competency.
AdvancedLoudConsistent
philosophy.md

How I Think

Data First, Always

A beautiful dashboard built on bad data is worse than no dashboard. I spend the time understanding the data before touching a visualisation tool.

Simple Until It Needs to Be Complex

A well-written SQL query beats a sprawling ML pipeline when SQL is what the problem needs. I reach for complexity only when simpler tools genuinely can't do the job.

Ask the Uncomfortable Question

The most valuable thing I do is often questioning the question. Most data requests are proxies for something deeper.

Ship, Then Improve

A working dashboard in front of users beats a perfect one that never ships. Get it out, watch how people use it, then make it better. Repeat.

© 2026 Old Man Yelling At — Data Science, AI & Cantankerousness

about.html:

home / about

OLD MAN
YELLING AT

Data Scientist · AI Practitioner · Professional Curmudgeon

The Yelling Man Behind the Data

I'm a data scientist with an unhealthy interest in finding the thing everyone else missed and an even unhealthier interest in telling them about it at length.

My work sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and AI — which mostly means I spend my time writing SQL, yelling at poorly-labelled axes, and building tools that make the messy stuff invisible to everyone else.

Outside the terminal, I'm poking at hardware projects, experimenting with LLMs, and maintaining a running list of planes flying too damn low over my house.

View Projects Get In Touch
location:Somewhere loud
role:Data Scientist / AI Practitioner
languages:Python, SQL (and yelling)
viz tools:Tableau, Apache Superset
ai/ml:LLMs, RAG, fine-tuning
hardware:Custom builds, edge compute
availability:Open to interesting problems
pet peeve:Truncated Y-axes
career.log

The Journey

Present
Data Scientist & AI Practitioner
Building data pipelines, dashboards, and AI tools. Yelling at incorrect data. Shipping projects that solve real problems.
Earlier
Analytics & Visualisation Work
Deep dives into Tableau and Superset. Learned that most business questions are actually three different questions in a trench coat.
Before That
Getting Into Data
SQL first, then Python, then the uncomfortable realisation that data is never as clean as anyone claims it is.
Origin Story
Hardware & Tinkering
Started with hardware, custom builds, and the kind of curiosity that ends with a room full of spare components. Still ongoing.
interests.json

What Gets Me Going

DATA
Data Engineering
Pipelines that don't break at 3am. Schemas that make sense. Dreams of a clean database.
LLM
AI & Language Models
Building with LLMs, breaking them, and figuring out where they genuinely help vs. where they just sound helpful.
HW
Hardware
Custom builds, tinkering, edge compute. The satisfaction of something physical that works.
VIZ
Data Visualisation
Turning numbers into something a human can actually understand. Occasionally succeeds.

© 2026 Old Man Yelling At — Data Science, AI & Cantankerousness
